name: A3F
shortDescription: This extension adds features to the built-in 3D.

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Use this when you want to set the initial properties of a 3D object.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: A3I

eventsFunctions.name: ChangeColor
eventsFunctions.description: Gradually changes the color of the 3D Light.

eventsFunctions.name: ChangeIntensity
eventsFunctions.description: Gradually changes the intensity of the 3D Light.

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Use this when you want to play sound from a 3D object.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: A3S

eventsFunctions.name: Play
eventsFunctions.description: Play sound from object center point.

eventsFunctions.name: StopAll
eventsFunctions.description: Stop all sounds emitted by this object.

eventsFunctions.name: LocalTranslateCameraDistance
eventsFunctions.description: Translates the camera along its local axis.

eventsFunctions.name: LocalTranslateCameraSpeed
eventsFunctions.description: Translates the camera along its local axis.

eventsFunctions.name: LocalRotateCameraAngle
eventsFunctions.description: Rotates the camera along its local axis.

eventsFunctions.name: LocalRotateCameraSpeed
eventsFunctions.description: Rotates the camera along its local axis.

eventsFunctions.name: LerpCamera
eventsFunctions.description:
Moves and/or rotates a 3D camera closer to another object’s position and rotation using linear and spherical linear interpolation.
Use the "factor" parameter to specify how much the camera should move and/or rotate (0 = no change, 0.5 = halfway, 1 = exact match).

eventsFunctions.name: OverlapObjects
eventsFunctions.description:
⚠️ Deprecated.
Overlay the object on the 3D object.
⚠️ For objects to overlap correctly, they must be on a different layer than the 3D object.

eventsFunctions.name: OverlapObjectsV2
eventsFunctions.description:
Overlay the object on the 3D object.
⚠️ For objects to overlap correctly, they must be on a different layer than the 3D object. In the layer properties, the 'Default camera behavior' under 'Camera positioning' must be set to 'Keep top-left corner fixed'.

eventsFunctions.name: LocalTranslateDistance
eventsFunctions.description: Translates the 3D object along its local axis.

eventsFunctions.name: LocalTranslateSpeed
eventsFunctions.description: Translates the 3D object along its local axis.

eventsFunctions.name: LocalRotateAngle
eventsFunctions.description: Rotates the 3D object along its local axis.

eventsFunctions.name: LocalRotateSpeed
eventsFunctions.description: Rotates the 3D object along its local axis.

eventsFunctions.name: LerpObject
eventsFunctions.description:
Moves and/or rotates a 3D object closer to another object’s position and rotation using linear and spherical linear interpolation.
Use the "factor" parameter to specify how much the object should move and/or rotate (0 = no change, 0.5 = halfway, 1 = exact match).

eventsFunctions.name: LookAtPosition
eventsFunctions.description: Rotates the 3D object so that its local +X direction points toward a specified 3D position.

eventsFunctions.name: LookAtObject
eventsFunctions.description: Rotates the 3D object so that its local +X direction points toward the target 3D object.

eventsFunctions.name: AddAxesHelper
eventsFunctions.description:
The axis helper is added to the 3D object.
Note that it is added to the center point, not the origin point.

eventsFunctions.name: ChangeBlendMode
eventsFunctions.description:
⚠️ Deprecated.
Change the blend mode of the 3D objects and all their children.
This affects all 3d objects that use the same material.

eventsFunctions.name: ChangeBlendModeV2
eventsFunctions.description:
Change the blend mode of the 3D objects and all their children.
This affects all 3d objects that use the same material.

eventsFunctions.name: Opacity
eventsFunctions.description: Returns the opacity of the 3D object, or the first opacity found among its children.

eventsFunctions.name: CheckOpacity
eventsFunctions.description:
Check the opacity of the 3D object, or the first opacity found among its children.
This condition can be used for non-3D objects as well, but in those cases the opacity is always compared as 255.

eventsFunctions.name: ChangeOpacity
eventsFunctions.description:
⚠️ Deprecated.
Change the opacity of the 3D objects and all their children.
This affects all 3D objects that use the same material.

eventsFunctions.name: ChangeOpacityV2
eventsFunctions.description: Change the opacity of the 3D objects and all their children.

eventsFunctions.name: ChangeShadow
eventsFunctions.description:
Change the shadow settings of 3D objects.
If both "Cast Shadow" and "Receive Shadow" are enabled, striped patterns are likely to occur in the shadow.

eventsFunctions.name: CheckDistance
eventsFunctions.fullName: ↔️Check the distance between two objects in 3D V1 (Deprecated)
eventsFunctions.description:
⚠️ Deprecated.
Checks the distance between two objects (origin points) in 3D.

eventsFunctions.name: DistanceObjects
eventsFunctions.description:
⚠️ Deprecated.
Returns the distance between objects in 3D. (Distance between origin points)
This expression always returns 0 when a 2D object is selected.

eventsFunctions.name: OutputHierarchy
eventsFunctions.description:
Outputs the hierarchy of the 3D object's children to the console.
It is recommended to check in the developer tools console, where line breaks and indentation are displayed, rather than in GDevelop's debugger console.
Use the following shortcut in the preview window to open the Developer Tools and select the Console tab:
Ctrl + Shift + I (Cmd + Option + I).

eventsFunctions.name: LerpTowardChild
eventsFunctions.description:
Linearly interpolates 3D objects toward a child of another 3D object.
The names of the children must be checked in advance using the "🛟Output children hierarchy" action or a 3D modeling tool.

eventsFunctions.name: ChildRotateAngle
eventsFunctions.description:
Rotate a child of the 3D objects along its local axes.
The names of the children must be checked in advance using the "🛟Output children hierarchy" action or a 3D modeling tool.
This can also be used to rotate bones.
Note that if a child is affected by a playing animation, it will override this change. In that case, please pause the animation.

eventsFunctions.name: ChildRotateSpeed
eventsFunctions.description:
Rotate a child of the 3D objects along its local axes.
The names of the children must be checked in advance using the "🛟Output children hierarchy" action or a 3D modeling tool.
This can also be used to rotate bones.
Note that if a child is affected by a playing animation, it will override this change. In that case, please pause the animation.

eventsFunctions.name: ChangeChildAngle
eventsFunctions.description:
Changes the angle of the 3D object's child along the child’s local axes.
The names of the children must be checked in advance using the "🛟Output children hierarchy" action or a 3D modeling tool.
This can also be used to rotate bones.
Note that if a child is affected by a playing animation, it will override this change. In that case, please pause the animation.

eventsFunctions.name: OutputMorphTarget
eventsFunctions.description:
Outputs the morph target names of the 3D object to the console.
It is recommended to check in the developer tools console, where line breaks and indentation are displayed, rather than in GDevelop's debugger console.
Use the following shortcut in the preview window to open the Developer Tools and select the Console tab:
Ctrl + Shift + I (Cmd + Option + I).

eventsFunctions.name: ChangeMorphTarget
eventsFunctions.description:
Change morph target values of 3D objects.
The names of the morph target must be checked in advance using the "🛟Output morph target names" action or a 3D modeling tool.
Note that if a morph target is affected by a playing animation, it will override this change. In that case, please pause the animation.

eventsFunctions.name: ResetMorphTargets
eventsFunctions.description:
Sets all morph target values of the 3D object to 0.
Note that if a morph target is affected by a playing animation, it will override this change. In that case, please pause the animation.

eventsFunctions.name: NormalizedDirection
eventsFunctions.description:
Stores the normalized direction vector of the 3D object in a variable.
The variable will be a structure variable with child variables x, y, and z.
Note that the variable will only store the value of the first referenced 3D object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetListenerForCamera
eventsFunctions.description:
Set a listener on the 3D camera.
Only one listener can exist in the scene at any time.
Each time the scene starts, the listener is automatically set on the base layer’s camera.

eventsFunctions.name: SetListenerForObject
eventsFunctions.description:
Set a listener at the center point of the first 3D object found.
This listener assumes that forward is X+, up is Z+, and right is Y+.
Therefore, if the object on which the listener is set is unrotated, it must have X+ as forward, Z+ as up, and Y+ as right; otherwise, the sound will not be heard from the correct direction.
Only one listener can exist in the scene at any time.
Each time the scene starts, the listener is automatically set on the base layer’s camera.

eventsFunctions.name: AngleToObjects
eventsFunctions.description:
Return the unsigned angle (in degrees, range 0 to 180) between the object's local X-axis and the direction to the target in 3D space.
This expression always returns 0 when a 2D object is selected.

eventsFunctions.name: DistanceObjectsV2
eventsFunctions.description:
Returns the distance between objects in 3D.
This expression always returns 0 when a 2D object is selected.

eventsFunctions.name: CheckAngleToObjects
eventsFunctions.description: Checks the unsigned angle (in degrees, range 0 to 180) between the object's local X-axis and the direction to the target in 3D space.

eventsFunctions.name: CheckDistanceV2
eventsFunctions.description: Checks the distance between two objects in 3D.

description:
3D features added by this extension:  
- Lighting
- Light color and intensity control
- Shadows
- 3D sound
- Local translation and rotation
- Blend modes
- Opacity
- Overlay objects
- Linear interpolation
- Look At
- Distance between 3D objects
- Angle between 3D objects
- Bone control
- Morph target control
- Access child objects of 3D objects
- Normalized direction
- Axes helper
- Light helper
- Shadow range helper
- Hierarchy output
